zh.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: 英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry於1516年出生在英國素福爾可賀瑟特時，他的父親Thomas Bacon40歲，母親Ann Rouse33歲。他於大約1552年與Lady Anne Drury在英國素福爾可賀瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1569年4月29日之前在英國素福爾可，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特。

zh-hans.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: 英国米德萊可倫敦 聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry于1516年出生在英国素福爾可賀瑟特时，他的父亲Thomas Bacon40岁，母亲Ann Rouse33岁。他于大约1552年与Lady Anne Drury在英国素福爾可賀瑟特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1569年4月29日之前在英国素福爾可，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦 聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry was born in 1516, in Hessett, Suffolk, England, his father, Thomas Bacon, was 40 and his mother, Ann Rouse, was 33. He married Lady Anne Drury about 1552, in Hessett, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died before 29 April 1569, in Suffolk, England, and was buried in London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex, England.

fr.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry est né en 1516, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Bacon, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Ann Rouse, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Lady Anne Drury environ 1552, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 29 avril 1569, à Suffolk, Angleterre, et a été enterré à London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex, Angleterre.

Er hat um 1552 in Hessett, Suffolk, England Lady Anne Drury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 29. Lui sposato Lady Anne Drury circa 1552, nel Hessett, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 29 aprile 1569, nel Suffolk, England, e fu sepolto a London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex, England.

ja.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン スト ダンスタン イン ス ウエスト
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewryが1516年にイギリススフォルクヘセットで生まれた時，父Thomas Baconは40歳，母Ann Rouseは33歳であった。約1552年にイギリススフォルクヘセットでLady Anne Druryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1569年4月29日以前にイギリススフォルクで死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン スト ダンスタン イン ス ウエストにて埋葬された。

ko.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 던스탠 인 테 웨스트
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry(이)가 1516년 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Bacon(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Rouse(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1552년 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 Lady Anne Drury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1569년 4월 29일 이전 영국 수폴크에서 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 던스탠 인 테 웨스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry nasceu em 1516, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Bacon, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Ann Rouse, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Anne Drury aproximadamente 1552, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 29 de abril de 1569, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.London St Dunstan in the West, Middlesex: лондон ст дунстань ин т вест, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry родился в 1516 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Bacon было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Rouse, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne Drury около 1552 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 29 апрель 1569 г. в местности суффолк, Англия и был похоронен в местности лондон ст дунстань ин т вест, миддлесекс, Англия.
